Figure 3: Indication of IDS corners available for testing	10

This protocol defines the internal bench test and inspection verification procedures, including the test method, DUT, sample selection, sample-size rationale, acceptance criteria, and test records required to verify the applicable cleanability requirements identified in the Traceability Matrix [Ref. 1], in accordance with the approved IDS Design Verification Plan [Ref. 3].

cells.value: When subjected 55.5% isopropyl alcohol (IPA), throughout the expected service life, the IDS materials and display glass shall maintain structural integrity without visible cracking or material degradation
604 - The system shall be cleanable using standard hospital cleaning and disinfectant agents without compromising its functional safety or integrity.
When subjected 27.3% ethanol, throughout the expected service life, the IDS materials and display glass shall maintain structural integrity without visible cracking or material degradation
When subjected 3% hydrogen peroxide, throughout the expected service life, the IDS materials and display glass shall maintain structural integrity without visible cracking or material degradation

When subjected to 1.1% sodium hypochlorite, throughout the expected service life, the IDS materials and display glass shall maintain structural integrity without visible cracking or material degradation
This protocol applies to chemical compatibility design verification of the IDS system components related to the requirements to be tested. These components are indicated below.
IDS Display UnitCMC AssemblyIDS Enclosure Cover Bracket

content.text: For each verification activity performed, the tester shall record execution-specific evidence sufficient to identify what was assessed, how it was assessed, and whether the acceptance criteria were met within a Test Record with a valid DRN as per SOP-001 Document & Data Control.

items.text: Test section and design requirement reference;
Date of assessment and tester name;
DUT identifiers, revision, serial number or unique sample ID, build state, and submitted-build configuration used;
Equipment identification and calibration or verification status where equipment status affects acceptance;
Exposure agent, exposure location, cycle timing, lighting condition, observations, photographs, and reviewed evidence;
Deviations, limitations, incomplete assessments, invalid test runs, and repeat-test rationale, where applicable; and
Pass/fail outcome against the applicable acceptance criteria.

content.text: Since all chemicals to be tested will be tested with the exact method a common test method has been developed in Section 3.5. Due to the nature of handling chemicals, the following guidelines must be adhered to:

items.text: When handling chemicals, it is mandatory to follow SOG-015 Chemicals document, wear required PPE (gloves, safety glasses, coat) and ensure the area of testing is within a well-ventilated area.
Each chemical exposure test will follow this test procedure exactly.
Different chemicals shall not be handled or used simultaneously.
Only one chemical type shall be present in the test area at a single time to avoid mixing or hazardous situations.
Chemicals and areas of the IDS to be tested must be labelled explicitly and immediately visible
No chemical shall be tested on an area of the IDS which has had a different chemical exposure to avoid mixing, and invalid results
In the event of accidental mixing of chemicals, the tester must immediately vacate the area and alert the WHS and Chemicals representative.

content.text: Mandatory Initial steps to follow before proceeding with any of the chemical solution mixing:

items.text: Confirm that chemical training has been completed
Confirm that SOG-015 has been read and understood
Read and understand the Safety Data Sheet for each chemical which can be found on the IDE server [Ref. 5] - [Ref. 8].
Storage of each chemical can be found within their risk assessment documents [Ref. 9]-[Ref. 12].
Ensure that the required PPE is being worn [Ref. 13].
Clean the working area, ensuring no other chemicals and obstructions are within the designated test area
Gather the chemical to be mixed
If the chemical concentration is not exactly the required testing concentration, then gather the deionised water for mixing
Ensure measuring beaker and funnel are clean and dry with no residual solution or chemicals within.

content.text: The following solution preparation steps define the calculation used to work out the volume needed of the chemical and deionised water to produce the required cleaning solution.
Note: Only one chemical mixing shall be mixed at a time, with clean and dry equipment, to prevent a hazardous chemical reaction.
Use the dilution equation to calculate the volume of the chosen chemical and deionised water to be added to the beaker to reach the required concentration for a certain volume:
Use the calculated volumes, for both the chemical in use and deionised water, when mixing the chemical with deionised water to achieve the required chemical concentration of the cleaning solution.
To create the 55.5% IPA cleaning solution, based on a final volume of 500ml, the required volumes of the chemical and deionised water would be:
To create the 27.3% Ethanol cleaning solution, based on a final volume of 500ml, the required volumes of the chemical and deionised water would be:
To create the 3% Hydrogen Peroxide cleaning solution, based on a final volume of 500ml, the required volumes of the chemical and deionised water would be:
To create the 1.1% Sodium Hypochlorite cleaning solution, based on a final volume of 500ml, the required volumes of the chemical and deionised water would be:
DUT, Chemical Test Area Designation, and Setup
The device under testing (DUT) is the latest approved and assembled IDS system (CMC-A-020)

items.text: The exposure location shall be one corner of the IDS display such that the exposed area includes both enclosure material and display glass (Figure 2). Each chemical verification test shall be performed on a separate corner, or a different IDS sample, to avoid accidental mixture of chemicals. Different chemicals shall not be tested at the same time, on the same exposure area, or in any manner that could allow accidental mixing of chemicals.

content.text: Figure 2: Indication of IDS corners available for testing

items.text: Select cleaning solution to be created55.5% IPA solution,27.3% ethanol,3% hydrogen peroxide solution, or1.1% sodium hypochlorite solution
Create cleaning solution as per Section 3.3.1.
Confirm the DUT is clean, dry, undamaged, and representative of the submitted build.

Define the mandatory exposure location as per Section 3.4.2Confirm exposure location has not been tested previously or has residual chemical solution.Each chemical verification test shall be performed on a separate area of the IDS, or a different IDS sample, to avoid accidental mixture of chemicals. Different chemicals shall not be tested at the same time, on the same exposure area, or in any manner that could allow accidental mixing of chemicals.
Apply tape around the designated area, demarcating the area to be cleaned and tested while preventing surrounding areas from exposure as shown in Figure 3.
Exposed Display Glass
Exposed Enclosure Material
Record DUT identification, revision of the system, cleaning solution being used, exposure location, control area, ambient conditions, and any pre-test condition.

content.text: Note: For the testing, orientation of the system is not critical, as long as the corner being cleaned can be viewed under a microscope, is accessible for wiping, and can be soaked in an easy manner.
Each cleaning solution will be tested in the same way and will follow a common method as presented below in Section
Test Aim: Determine whether IDS materials and display glass are compatible with cleaning chemicals after the defined wiping and soaked exposure sequence.DUT & Sample Selection & Justification
The DUT shall be one representative IDS assembly [Ref. 2].
Figure 4: Representative IDS Assembly For Testing

cells.value: DUT shall be clean, dry, undamaged, production-equivalent, and representative of the submitted build. The mandatory exposure location shall include both enclosure material and display glass at the IDS display corner as defined in Section:

Type test of the submitted IDS assembly under the specified cleaning solution wiping and soaked exposure sequence. One DUT verifies the submitted material, display glass, surface finish, assembly interface, and exposure location under the defined worst-case local contact condition.
Fresh paper towels for soaking with chemical solution
Suitable for covering the soaked cloth and minimising evaporation
Suitable for securing chosen covering material to the DUT without obstructing the exposure area
Suitable for recording 10 minute wiping and greater than 24 hour exposure durations
Suitable for before and after photographs

content.text: SAFETY WARNING: Do not mix cleaning or disinfecting agents. Mixing chemicals such as IPA, ethanol, hydrogen peroxide, and sodium hypochlorite can generate hazardous or toxic vapours. Always follow SOG-015 Chemicals.
Take all precautions necessary to prevent exposure.

items.text: Perform only one chemical exposure test at a time unless physically separated and controlled to prevent mixing of chemicals.
Wear the required PPE (gloves, safety glasses, coat)
Perform test in ventilated environment

content.text: For the following method, ensure to record:

items.text: Chemical identity
Active ingredient percentage within the test solution
Exposure start and finish times for each wiping and soaked exposure
Photographs before and after test
Observations
Deviations throughout the test
Place the DUT and designated corner under the microscope.
Take images of the DUT materials before cleaning, ensuring:high image qualitymaterial surface finish can be identifiedany blemishes or marks within the designated area is captured and identified
Position the IDS in a stable position before beginning with the cleaning process.

Saturate a clean lint-free cloth with the specified chemical to be tested. Continuously wipe the defined exposure area for 10 minutes and ensure that both the display glass and enclosure material maintain a visibly wet surface, within the demarcated area, throughout the wiping period.
Saturate multiple paper cloths with the specified chemical. Place the cloths directly over the defined exposure area ensuring contact over the entire demarcated area.
Reframe from exposing areas outside of the demarcated to the soaked clothes
Ensure that the paper cloths are visibly saturated and wet with the solution.

Cover the cloths with a plastic bag or polyethylene film and secure the covering to the DUT using tape.The tape should align with the taped used for demarcating the area to be cleaned. Ensure that the tape is sealed around the edges of the plastic covering to minimise evaporation while maintaining continuous contact between the cloth and DUT.
Leave undisturbed for > 24 hours.
Remove the both sets of tape and the soaked clothe and leave DUT to dry
Once Dry, place the DUT under the microscope and take images of the identical positions that were captured in the pre-clean inspection
Additionally, take images of the border between the designated area that was cleaned and the are of the DUT that was under the initial tape

Examine the exposed area and adjacent unexposed control area for cracking, clouding, swelling, softening, deformation, and delamination, and any other visible material degradation. Take photographs and notes on any observations.

content.text: PASS: No visible cracking or signs of material degradation of the cleaned area.

cells.value: Conditions to be met for the verification activity to pass.
Physical IDS assembly or defined configuration assessed during verification.
IDS assembly that is production-equivalent and representative of the submitted build state.
Risk-based requirement classification recorded in the Traceability Matrix.
The specific hardware, assembly state, labels, materials, surface finish, accessories, and settings relevant to the requirement being verified.
Defined procedure used to perform the verification activity.
Completed record of setup, reviewed files, observations, photographs, deviations, results, and pass/fail outcome.
Deterministic verification of design compliance under defined conditions where the expected outcome is not intended to demonstrate manufacturing population capability.
